target.name: Anthropic

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $7.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: OpenAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $300B+

private.name: Anysphere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $29.3B

private.name: Neuralink
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $9.7B

private.name: Groq
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $7B

data.narrative:
Anthropic, a private-stage AI & Software company, was most recently valued at $183B following $13B+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four same-sector, same-stage comparables: OpenAI ($300.0B), Anysosphere ($29.3B), Neuralink ($9.7B), and Groq ($7.0B).

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given data availability, yields an implied valuation of $19.5B. This median reflects the substantial dispersion within the AI & Software private frontier-tech space, where valuations range from $7.0B to $300.0B. The four-peer sample captures heterogeneity in company maturity, business model, and market positioning within the sector.

The triangulated valuation range is $16.6B (low), $19.5B (median), and $22.4B (high). The material gap between this $16.6B–$22.4B range and Anthropic's stated $183B valuation warrants scrutiny. Key uncertainties include whether the peer set adequately reflects Anthropic's proprietary capabilities, customer concentration, and path to profitability—factors not captured in valuation multiples alone. The single-method result, limited peer comparability, and the extreme width of the raw peer range ($7.0B–$300.0B) counsel caution in relying on this analysis as dispositive. Additional methods triangulation (discounted cash flow, comparable transaction multiples) would be required to narrow the valuation band.
